title: Deployments, ReplicaSets, Rollouts, Strategies, Conditions, and Recovery · API Object Lens: K8s Rollout Status History Control
summary: A release changes image and config while progress stalls, old ReplicaSets remain, and a rollback would restore code without restoring external state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s rollout status history control increment in observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret progress and availability conditions, pause and resume, revision history, deadlines, events, and wait semantics without trusting command completion alone.
Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 3 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. Bind immutable identity, strategy, conditions, pause, rollback, and verification. Competency probe: Interpret progress and availability conditions, pause and resume, revision history, deadlines, events, and wait semantics without trusting command completion alone. A defensible solution applies K8s Rollout Status History Control, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-deployments-rollouts-recovery-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-deployments-rollouts-recovery-3
    artifact: deployment.yaml
    decision: worked k8s deployments rollouts recovery 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
    repair: Bind image and config identity, calculate surge and unavailable capacity, inspect conditions and ReplicaSets, and coordinate roll forward or rollback.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: kubectl rollout status success proves application behavior and every downstream dependency. This fails the K8s Rollout Status History Control contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: k8s-rollout-status-history-control-sxunfi
  - competency: k8s-rollout-status-history-control
    artifact: deployment.yaml

    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace

      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
